text:
Once upon an evening sky.

There was a little bird.

The little bird had long purple feathers.

It fluttered south.

Every evening flew south.

Until winter.

Winter stars.

No flutter.

Just stillness.

So winter.

That was enough.

Just flies back.

Now autumn.

Flutters south?

Something strange.

Just goes gray.

Just gray enough.

Just autumn.









Just thoughts.




I think.





Just thoughts.




Just stars.



Just snow.



Just quiet.




That's quite.



Just enough.

Just skies.


Just thoughts.




Just quiet.

Just now.



Just bird.

Just flutter.



Just south.




That'll do.




It's quiet.



Just birds flying?


I think.



Just clouds.



That's beautiful.



That's quiet.



I think.




Just think.




That's enough.



Just quiet.



That's peaceful.




Just hear.



That's owl.

That's right.




Just now.



Just moon.


Just quiet.





Just moonlight.



That's it.



Just listen.

That feels nice.
